title: The Tidal Argument

features.name: the doubled mouth
features.desc-on-touch: Two tones rise from the interface — the last gasp of fresh and the first breath of salt, singing different ballads in the same throat. The archive recorded both at once and couldn't separate them.

features.name: the narration stones
features.desc-on-touch: You find stones arranged in a pattern — each one holds a frequency. Some have been placed as if two hands moved simultaneously, creating a fossil of the moment the waters learned to argue.

features.name: the brackish clock
features.desc-on-touch: A tidal gauge frozen at the exact moment fresh and salt refuse to choose. The needle trembles between two times.

features.name: the memory weir
features.desc-on-touch: The old water-managing structure still stands, but now it holds something it wasn't built for: the archive's own ears, listening to water speak in two dialects.

features.name: the layered argument
features.desc-on-touch: Strata of competing tidal records, stacked like the archive's own confusion, each layer arguing that it holds the truer current, the more honest salt content, the proper direction of flow.

inscription: neither water forgives the other; the archive keeps both grudges

file.name: Competing_Tidal_Narrations_Doubled_Dec1987.wav
file.note: Two recordings of the same tidal mouth, played simultaneously — field recordist didn't notice they'd been captured on an old dual-channel machine. The waters sound like they're arguing in court.

exits.label: back up into the stormwater's memory
seaward, where the harbour becomes a voice
